chapter_title.ar: باب زَكَاةِ مَالِ الْمَمْلُوكِ وَ الْمُكَاتَبِ وَ الْمَجْنُونِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 24 – Zakāt on the wealth of the owned slaves, and the contracted slaves, and the insane

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/78)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/78)

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Abu Al Bakhtary,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘There is no Zakāt regarding the wealth of the contracted slaves’.
en.sarwar: 5. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn al-Khashshab from Ali ibn al-Husayn from Muhammad ibn abu Hamzah from ‘Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a slave who has an asset; if there is Zakat on it. He (the Imam) said, ‘No, Zakat is not due on it.’ I then asked, ‘Is his owner required to pay Zakat?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘No, because it (asset) cannot go to his owner and it is not for the slave.’”
